title: becium aureoviride var. lupotoense
intro: Becium aureoviride var. lupotoense, also known as 'Lupoto' or 'Lupoto Bush', is a plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family, native to Tanzania and Kenya. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. Becium aureoviride var. lupotoense can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to harness its potential health benefits.
